mnemonics: Global Uniqueness: Scope Resolution Points to the One True Definition (::)
keyPoints: The :: operator is used to access a global variable when a local variable of the same name exists.
It is used to define member functions outside their class declaration.
It is used to access static members or nested types of a class.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to use the ClassName:: prefix when defining a member function outside the class body.
Confusing the scope resolution operator (::) with the member access operators (. or ->).
